events.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
events.description: Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday.

events.title: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
events.description: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazard Half of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report. Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says

events.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
events.description: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and Ukraine. Continue reading...

events.title: Police failed to act on repeated warnings about a violent man before he killed a toddler. But that’s not the most shocking thing about this case
events.description: The Mason Lee case was not an anomaly. Queensland’s police and coronial systems work as designed – and that’s the core of the current crisis Nine months before the death of the toddler Mason Jet Lee , a woman called police to warn them about the boy’s killer, William Andrew O’Sullivan. They placed a flag on his file labelling the woman’s claims “vexatious”. But that’s not the most shocking part of this story. Police already had evidence O’Sullivan was suicidal and homicidal. Months earlier they’d gone with him to a hospital, seeking mental health treatment, and while there he threatened to “skin” the woman and kill children. But that’s also not the most alarming thing. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Police failed to act on repeated warnings about a violent man before he killed a toddler. But that’s not the most shocking thing about this case

events.title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
events.description: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
events.description: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted messaging apps such as Signal, Telegram and WhatsApp by “bad actors” and paid by the networks to carry out the attacks. Shooters are required to film their attacks in order to get paid. Continue reading...

events.title: Starbucks Korea to temporarily shut all stores for history lesson after bungled coffee promotion

More than 2,000 stores will temporarily close at 3pm on 22 June, the company said, so staff can watch recorded lectures on modern Korean history and engage in “social sensitivity” training. events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
events.description: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977 The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief. Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. Continue reading...

events.title: Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness
events.description: Instructors hurled Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas into 40-metre abyss without attaching safety equipment A 21-year-old woman who died when two rope-jumping instructors threw her from a bridge without first harnessing her to security equipment has been buried in Brazil ’s São Paulo state. Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas was rope jumping on Saturday at Ponte do Esqueleto, an abandoned bridge in the municipality of Limeira where tourists practise extreme sports. The young woman, who aspired to become a physical education teacher, had asked to be launched from the bridge airplane-style, with two instructors hoisting her above their shoulders as she spread out her arms. events.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago

events.title: Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shield of immunity

events.title: Sigh of relief in Africa as the Strait of Hormuz 'reopens'
events.description: A potential US-Iran agreement could lower energy-, fertilizer- and food prices in Africa if the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens. Oil exporters such as Nigeria and Angola might have to cope with less revenue, experts say.

events.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
events.description: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

events.title: Can Hormuz be bypassed? Past shows commercial viability may simply not be there, analyst says
events.description: On Tuesday, leaders at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains were seeking to identify alternative routes to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, which US President Donald Trump said would be "completely open" on Friday. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Maisoon H Kafafy, Senior Advisor to the Atlantic Council’s Middle East programme, says that in previous instances of oil supply disruptions "the commercial viability [of building alternative infrastructure] was simply not there and the economics of the upfront investment into this projects were perhaps too large to justify".

events.title: Did Macron ‘snub’ Trump at the G7 summit? Viral video lacks context
events.description: With the G7 summit beginning Tuesday in France, social media users claimed French leader Emmanuel Macron deliberately "humiliated" Donald Trump by not being there to greet him upon his arrival at the airport. Many posts shared a video showing Trump disembarking an aircraft and later appearing unsmiling during a handshake with Macron, suggesting the two events were linked. However, the viral footage omits important context.

events.title: Why Ukraine could now have fresh hope of vital US support
events.description: The official G7 agenda started on Tuesday morning with a two-hour session on Ukraine.

events.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince jailed for two rapes
events.description: The stepson of Norway's Crown Prince Haakon has been jailed for two rapes - including one in the basement of his family's home.

events.title: Nations in Kenya urge quick execution of a key treaty protecting oceans
events.description: African and Commonwealth nations have called for a swift implementation of a landmark treaty protecting the high seas

events.title: German president says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea
events.description: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea that could endanger freedom of navigation as happened in the Strait of Hormuz
events.ticker_label: 💥 German president says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea

events.title: Monumental cave art on Paris' oldest bridge finally opens, as the public steps and sniffs inside
events.description: French street artist JR has transformed Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into an artificial cavern

events.title: Uffizi Gallery unveils new arrangement for Botticelli's 'Birth of Venus' and 'Primavera'
events.description: The Uffizi Gallery has repositioned Sandro Botticelli’s masterpieces “The Birth of Venus” and “Primavera.”

events.title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising, but challenges linger
events.description: South Africa marks the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising by students, a pivotal moment in the fight against apartheid

events.title: Brazil's top court convicts son of former President Bolsonaro for coercion
events.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court convicted former lawmaker Eduardo Bolsonaro on Tuesday for coercion

events.title: Swedish court imprisons man convicted of pressuring his wife to sell sexual services
events.description: A Swedish court has convicted a man of charges including aggravated procurement for pressuring his wife into selling sex to a large number of other men

events.title: UN-backed court opens trial of former Central African Republic president Bozizé
events.description: A U.N.-backed court in Central African Republic has opened the trial of former President François Bozizé

events.title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia’s shadow fleet
events.description: Britain’s armed forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker, the Smyrtos, in the English Channel on Sunday

events.title: Luigi Mangione supporter working for far-left DC mayoral candidate likened to Mamdani
events.description: Makia Green, political director for D.C. mayoral candidate Janeese Lewis George, called for accused assassin Luigi Mangione to be freed and pardoned.

events.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained before murder trial
events.description: Teen accused of killing stepsister Anna Kepner on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained after judge finds no release conditions can protect community.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's father rips former Frisco schools chief as 'spineless' over Karmelo Anthony graduation
events.description: Father of slain teen Austin Metcalf slams former Frisco ISD superintendent, claiming he allowed killer Karmelo Anthony to graduate after the stabbing.

events.title: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at vacation resort, tortured them at boat ramp: FBI
events.description: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at gunpoint from Margaritaville Hollywood Beach Resort in Florida, tortured them and demanded money, officials say.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
